title: 0.92x decreased risk of Atrial Fibrillation and cardioembolic stroke
summary: 0.92x decreased risk of Atrial Fibrillation and cardioembolic stroke.
detail: This is the normal version which makes the heart form properly and makes people slightly less likely to get Atrial Fibrillation (quivering of the top chambers of the heart caused by chaotic electrical signals) and less likely to have a cardioembolic stroke (blocked blood flow to the brain). But also check the other SNP nearby (rs2200733) which affects Atrial Fibrillation even more. 23andMe gave this a 4 star confidence rating as it has been heavily studied. It has been confirmed in Europeans.
whyItMatters: SNPedia has a genotype-specific page for this result, which can add consumer-facing context alongside primary evidence sources.

title: Probably Benign Rett Syndrome genotype: Feedback Welcome
summary: Probably Benign Rett Syndrome genotype: Feedback Welcome
detail: This is reported in another database as a mutation leading to Rett Syndrome, however, see note below and at rs61754424. Please feel free to contact info@snpedia.com if you have personal information about this genotype and it's relation (if any) to Rett Syndrome in yourself or your family. Note: Last Updated November 2016: this genotype has been observed in data from two 23andMe unrelated users as well as two Ancestry.com users, all of whom have indicated they have no reason to believe they or anyone in their family had ever had any Rett syndrome features. We are provisionally concluding that this genotype is benign, despite what other databases may say (or have said) about it.
